name: optimize-performance
description: End-to-end FPS orchestration for a game that runs but stutters — not a crash. Applies the Fabric performance stack + memory + in-game options + verify to an instance that already launches. Use when the user complains of lag, low FPS, stutter, or freezing ("卡","掉帧","优化","提升性能","太卡了"); coordinates create-instance/set-memory/mods-install/game-options/launch-and-verify — if the game instead crashes or won't start, that is diagnose-crash.
body:
# Optimize Minecraft performance

Goal: measurably improve FPS / reduce stutter. Orchestrate the skills below; add only
scenario-specific judgment calls here.

## Flow
1. `instance(action=details)` — check the loader. Sodium needs **Fabric** (or NeoForge w/
   port); if vanilla/Forge-only, ask whether to run `create-instance` first.
2. `set-memory` — modded default 4–6 GB, vanilla ~2 GB; never more than ~half the
   machine's physical RAM.
3. `mods-install` — Fabric perf stack, confirm MC-version compatibility: Sodium
   (core FPS), Lithium (game-logic), FerriteCore (memory), EntityCulling (culling), Iris
   only if shaders wanted.
4. `game-options` — render distance 8–12, disable fancy graphics, VSync off if
   input lag matters.
5. `launch-and-verify` — ask if FPS improved; if not, check for a heavy mod (shaders,
   Distant Horizons) to disable.

## Don't
- Sodium requires Fabric — don't install it on a non-Fabric instance.
- Don't pair OptiFine with Fabric performance mods (incompatible).
